## Part 3: Working with Data

We'll start with a guided example using student assessment data.

- [DC Public Schools Student Assessment Results](data/DC_Public_Schools_Student_Assessment_Results.csv)

### Step 1: Load Required Packages






::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(tidyverse)
df <- read_csv("../data/DC_Public_Schools_Student_Assessment_Results.csv")

# View the first few rows
head(df)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 6 × 23
  OBJECTID SCHOOL_YEAR SCHOOL_WARD SCHOOL_CODE SCHOOL_NAME     LEA_CODE LEA_NAME
     <dbl> <chr>       <chr>             <dbl> <chr>              <dbl> <chr>   
1        1 2014-15     Ward 8              217 Achievement Pr…      155 Achieve…
2        2 2014-15     Ward 8              217 Achievement Pr…      155 Achieve…
3        3 2014-15     Ward 8              217 Achievement Pr…      155 Achieve…
4        4 2014-15     Ward 8              217 Achievement Pr…      155 Achieve…
5        5 2014-15     Ward 8              217 Achievement Pr…      155 Achieve…
6        6 2014-15     Ward 8              217 Achievement Pr…      155 Achieve…
# ℹ 16 more variables: ASSESSMENT_TYPE <chr>, SUBJECT <chr>,
#   TESTED_GRADE_SUBJECT <chr>, GRADE_OF_ENROLLMENT <chr>, SUBGROUP <chr>,
#   SUBGROUP_VALUE <chr>, PERCENT_MEETING_OR_EXCEEDING_E <chr>,
#   PERCENT_LEVEL_3_ <chr>, PERCENT_LEVEL_1 <chr>, PERCENT_LEVEL_2 <chr>,
#   PERCENT_LEVEL_3 <chr>, PERCENT_LEVEL_4 <chr>, PERCENT_LEVEL_5 <chr>,
#   TOTAL_NUMBER_VALID_TEST_TAKERS <chr>, CREATED_DATE <lgl>,
#   LAST_EDITED_DATE <lgl>
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
# Check structure
glimpse(df)
```
